subsection.heading: General rule

paragraph.content: an amount is excluded from gross income under subsection (a) of section 108 (relating to discharge of indebtedness), and
under subsection (b)(2)(E), (b)(5), or (c)(1) of section 108, any portion of such amount is to be applied to reduce basis,

subsection.continuation: then such portion shall be applied in reduction of the basis of any property held by the taxpayer at the beginning of the taxable year following the taxable year in which the discharge occurs.

subsection.heading: Amount and properties determined under regulations

content.p: The amount of reduction to be applied under subsection (a) (not in excess of the portion referred to in subsection (a)), and the particular properties the bases of which are to be reduced, shall be determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

paragraph.heading: Limitation in title 11 case or insolvency
paragraph.chapeau: In the case of a discharge to which subparagraph (A) or (B) of section 108(a)(1) applies, the reduction in basis under subsection (a) of this section shall not exceed the excess of—

subparagraph.content: the aggregate of the bases of the property held by the taxpayer immediately after the discharge, over
the aggregate of the liabilities of the taxpayer immediately after the discharge.

paragraph.continuation: The preceding sentence shall not apply to any reduction in basis by reason of an election under section 108(b)(5).

paragraph.heading: Certain reductions may only be made in the basis of depreciable property

content.p: Any amount which under subsection (b)(5) or (c)(1) of section 108 is to be applied to reduce basis shall be applied only to reduce the basis of depreciable property held by the taxpayer.

For purposes of this section, the term “depreciable property” means any property of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation, but only if a basis reduction under subsection (a) will reduce the amount of depreciation or amortization which otherwise would be allowable for the period immediately following such reduction.

For purposes of this section, any interest of a partner in a partnership shall be treated as depreciable property to the extent of such partner’s proportionate interest in the depreciable property held by such partnership. The preceding sentence shall apply only if there is a corresponding reduction in the partnership’s basis in depreciable property with respect to such partner.

subparagraph.heading: Special rule in case of affiliated group
subparagraph.chapeau: For purposes of this section, if—

clause.content: a corporation holds stock in another corporation (hereinafter in this subparagraph referred to as the “subsidiary”), and
such corporations are members of the same affiliated group which file a consolidated return under section 1501 for the taxable year in which the discharge occurs,

subparagraph.continuation: then such stock shall be treated as depreciable property to the extent that such subsidiary consents to a corresponding reduction in the basis of its depreciable property.

subparagraph.heading: Election to treat certain inventory as depreciable property

content.p: At the election of the taxpayer, for purposes of this section, the term “depreciable property” includes any real property which is described in section 1221(a)(1).

An election under clause (i) shall be made on the taxpayer’s return for the taxable year in which the discharge occurs or at such other time as may be permitted in regulations prescribed by the Secretary. Such an election, once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary.

subparagraph.heading: Special rules for qualified real property business indebtedness
subparagraph.chapeau: In the case of any amount which under section 108(c)(1) is to be applied to reduce basis—

clause.content: depreciable property shall only include depreciable real property for purposes of subparagraphs (A) and (C),

in the case of property taken into account under section 108(c)(2)(B), the reduction with respect to such property shall be made as of the time immediately before disposition if earlier than the time under subsection (a).

paragraph.heading: Special rules for qualified farm indebtedness

subparagraph.heading: In general
subparagraph.chapeau: Any amount which under subsection (b)(2)(E) of section 108 is to be applied to reduce basis and which is attributable to an amount excluded under subsection (a)(1)(C) of section 108—

clause.content: shall be applied only to reduce the basis of qualified property held by the taxpayer, and

clause.chapeau: shall be applied to reduce the basis of qualified property in the following order:

subclause.content: First the basis of qualified property which is depreciable property.
Second the basis of qualified property which is land used or held for use in the trade or business of farming.
Then the basis of other qualified property.

content.p: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “qualified property” has the meaning given to such term by section 108(g)(3)(C).
Rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (C), (D), and (E) of paragraph (3) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph and section 108(g).

paragraph.heading: Reduction not to be made in exempt property

content.p: In the case of an amount excluded from gross income under section 108(a)(1)(A), no reduction in basis shall be made under this section in the basis of property which the debtor treats as exempt property under section 522 of title 11 of the United States Code.

paragraph.heading: Reductions in basis not treated as dispositions

content.p: For purposes of this title, a reduction in basis under this section shall not be treated as a disposition.

subparagraph.content: any property the basis of which is reduced under this section and which is neither section 1245 property nor section 1250 property shall be treated as section 1245 property, and
any reduction under this section shall be treated as a deduction allowed for depreciation.

content.p: For purposes of section 1250(b), the determination of what would have been the depreciation adjustments under the straight line method shall be made as if there had been no reduction under this section.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1998—Subsec. (a)(2). Pub. L. 105–206 substituted “(b)(2)(E)” for “(b)(2)(D)”.
1996—Subsec. (b)(4)(A). Pub. L. 104–188 substituted “subsection (b)(2)(E)” for “subsection (b)(2)(D)”.
Subsec. (b)(3)(A). Pub. L. 103–66, § 13150(c)(7), inserted “or (c)(1)” after “subsection (b)(5)”.
Subsec. (b)(3)(F). Pub. L. 103–66, § 13150(c)(8), added subpar. (F).
1990—Subsec. (b)(4)(C). Pub. L. 101–508 substituted “subparagraphs” for “subparagraph”.

1988—Subsec. (b)(4). Pub. L. 100–647 substituted “Special rules for” for “Ordering rule in the case of” in heading, and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any amount which is excluded from gross income under section 108(a) by reason of the discharge of qualified farm indebtedness (within the meaning of section 108(g)(2)) and which under subsection (b) of section 108 is to be applied to reduce basis shall be applied—
“(A) first to reduce the tax attributes described in section 108(b)(2) (other than subparagraph (D) thereof),
“(B) then to reduce basis of property other than property described in subparagraph (C), and
“(C) then to reduce the basis of land used or held for use in the trade or business of farming.”
Subsec. (b)(3)(A). Pub. L. 99–514, § 822(b)(5), struck out “or (c)(1)(A)” after “subsection (b)(5)”.
Subsec. (b)(4). Pub. L. 99–514, § 405(b), added par. (4).

1980—Pub. L. 96–589 generally revised and expanded the section to specify the amount of reduction of basis of property under different subsections of section 108 of this title and the property to which such reduction is applicable, and provided for recapture of reductions for purposes of gains from depreciable property.
1976—Pub. L. 94–455, §§ 1906(b)(13)(A), 1951(c)(1), substituted “section 108” for “section 108(a)” in three places and struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1999 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 106–170 applicable to any instrument held, acquired, or entered into, any transaction entered into, and supplies held or acquired on or after Dec. 17, 1999, see section 532(d) of Pub. L. 106–170, set out as a note under section 170 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1996 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 104–188 effective as if included in the provision of the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1993, Pub. L. 103–66, §§ 13001–13444, to which such amendment relates, see section 1703(o) of Pub. L. 104–188, set out as a note under section 39 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1993 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 103–66 applicable to discharges after Dec. 31, 1992, in taxable years ending after such date, see section 13150(d) of Pub. L. 103–66, set out as a note under section 108 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1988 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 100–647 effective, except as otherwise provided, as if included in the provision of the Tax Reform Act of 1986, Pub. L. 99–514, to which such amendment relates, see section 1019(a) of Pub. L. 100–647, set out as a note under section 1 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1986 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by section 405(b) of Pub. L. 99–514 applicable to discharges of indebtedness occurring after Apr. 9, 1986, in taxable years ending after such date, see section 405(c) of Pub. L. 99–514, set out as a note under section 108 of this title.
Amendment by section 822(b)(4), (5) of Pub. L. 99–514 applicable to discharges after Dec. 31, 1986, see section 822(c) of Pub. L. 99–514, set out as a note under section 108 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1980 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 96–589 applicable to transactions which occur after Dec. 31, 1980, other than transactions which occur in a proceeding in a bankruptcy case or similar judicial proceeding or in a proceeding under Title 11 commencing on or after Dec. 31, 1980, with an exception permitting the debtor to make the amendment applicable to transactions occurring after Sept. 30, 1979 in a specified manner, see section 7(a) and (f) of Pub. L. 96–589, set out as a note under section 108 of this title.
